---
title: "Advanced Shiny Dashboards"
subtitle: ": Deployment and Polish for Assignment 2"
date: 04/29/2025
date-format: long
author:
  - name: Hyesop Shin
    orcid: 0000-0003-2637-7933
    email: hyesop.shin@auckland.ac.nz
    affiliations:
      - name: University of Auckland
        address: 23 Symonds Street
        city: Auckland
        postal-code: 1010
format: 
  revealjs:
      logo: Figs/uoa-logo.png
      css: logo.css
      slide-number: true
      show-slide-number: print
editor: source
include-in-header:
  - text: |
      <style type="text/css">
      ul li ul li {
        font-size: 0.7em;
      }
      </style>
---

## Recap of Previous Lectures

* Shiny basics (UI + Server + App)
* Network analysis with OSMnx
* Routing with r5py
* Reactive programming

## Today's Lecture

::: {.incremental}
* Temporal analysis in dashboards
* Advanced reactive patterns
* **Shinylive deployment**
* Assignment 2 final requirements
* Debugging and testing
:::

## Assignment 2 Due This Week!

**<span style="color:red">Due: Thursday 8 May, 5pm</span>**

::: {.incremental}
* Worth **35% of your final grade**
* Must be deployed with shinylive
* Multi-page dashboard
* Interactive map required
* Today we'll cover deployment!
:::

## What Makes a Good Dashboard?

![](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:1400/1*6RvBKT8nFDz_T5dHZOPV2A.png){fig-align=center width=70%}

::: {.incremental}
* **Clear purpose** - What question does it answer?
* **Intuitive interface** - Easy to understand
* **Fast response** - No 30-second loading times!
* **Good design** - Professional appearance
:::

# Part 1: Temporal Analysis

## Working with Time Data

```python
import pandas as pd
from datetime import datetime

# Load pedestrian data
df = pd.read_csv('pedestrian_counts.csv')

# Convert to datetime
df['timestamp'] = pd.to_datetime(df['timestamp'])

# Extract time components
df['hour'] = df['timestamp'].dt.hour
df['day_of_week'] = df['timestamp'].dt.day_name()
df['month'] = df['timestamp'].dt.month
df['is_weekend'] = df['day_of_week'].isin(['Saturday', 'Sunday'])
```

## Temporal Aggregation

::: {style="font-size: 90%;"}
```python
# Hourly averages
hourly_avg = df.groupby('hour')['count'].mean()

# Daily patterns
daily_avg = df.groupby('day_of_week')['count'].mean()

# By sensor and hour
pivot = df.pivot_table(
    values='count',
    index='hour',
    columns='sensor_id',
    aggfunc='mean'
)
```
:::

## Time Slider in Shiny

::: {style="font-size: 80%;"}
```python
from shiny import ui, render, reactive
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.h2("Temporal Patterns"),
    ui.input_slider(
        "hour",
        "Hour of Day:",
        min=0,
        max=23,
        value=12,
        step=1
    ),
    ui.output_plot("hourly_plot")
)

def server(input, output, session):
    @output
    @render.plot
    def hourly_plot():
        # Filter by selected hour
        hour_data = df[df['hour'] == input.hour()]
        
        # Create plot
        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))
        hour_data.groupby('sensor_id')['count'].mean().plot(kind='bar', ax=ax)
        ax.set_title(f"Average Counts at {input.hour()}:00")
        ax.set_ylabel("Pedestrian Count")
        return fig
```
:::

## Date Range Selector

::: {style="font-size: 85%;"}
```python
app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.input_date_range(
        "dates",
        "Date Range:",
        start="2024-01-01",
        end="2024-12-31"
    ),
    ui.output_plot("time_series")
)

def server(input, output, session):
    @reactive.calc
    def filtered_data():
        # Get date range
        start, end = input.dates()
        
        # Filter data
        mask = (df['timestamp'] >= start) & (df['timestamp'] <= end)
        return df[mask]
    
    @output
    @render.plot
    def time_series():
        data = filtered_data()
        
        # Daily aggregation
        daily = data.groupby(data['timestamp'].dt.date)['count'].sum()
        
        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 6))
        daily.plot(ax=ax)
        ax.set_title("Daily Pedestrian Counts")
        return fig
```
:::

## Your Turn: Temporal Dashboard

::: {style="font-size: 110%;"}
**<span style="color:blue">Task</span>**: Create a dashboard page with:

1. Hour slider (0-23)
2. Day of week selector
3. Plot showing filtered data

**Bonus**: Add weekend/weekday toggle
:::

# Part 2: Advanced Reactive Patterns

## Reactive Calculations {.smaller}

**<span style="color:blue">Problem</span>**: Expensive operations running multiple times

```python
# ❌ Bad - processes data twice!
@output
@render.plot
def plot1():
    data = process_large_dataset(input.filter())  # Slow!
    return create_plot(data)

@output
@render.table
def table1():
    data = process_large_dataset(input.filter())  # Slow again!
    return data
```

---

### Solution: @reactive.calc

::: {style="font-size: 85%;"}
```python
# ✅ Good - processes data once!
@reactive.calc
def processed_data():
    """Process data once, use many times."""
    return process_large_dataset(input.filter())

@output
@render.plot
def plot1():
    data = processed_data()  # Uses cached result
    return create_plot(data)

@output
@render.table
def table1():
    data = processed_data()  # Same cached result
    return data
```
:::

## Reactive Events

**<span style="color:blue">Problem</span>**: Update only on button click, not on every input change

::: {style="font-size: 85%;"}
```python
app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.input_text("location", "Location:"),
    ui.input_numeric("radius", "Radius (m):", value=1000),
    ui.input_action_button("go", "Calculate!", class_="btn-primary"),
    ui.output_plot("map")
)

def server(input, output, session):
    @reactive.event(input.go)  # Only when button clicked!
    def analysis():
        # This runs only when button clicked
        # Not when location or radius change
        return calculate_expensive_analysis(
            input.location(),
            input.radius()
        )
    
    @output
    @render.plot
    def map():
        result = analysis()
        return create_map(result)
```
:::

## Conditional Panels

**<span style="color:blue">Show/hide UI based on selections</span>**

::: {style="font-size: 80%;"}
```python
app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.input_radio_buttons(
        "analysis_type",
        "Analysis Type:",
        choices=["Simple", "Advanced"]
    ),
    
    # Only show advanced options when "Advanced" selected
    ui.panel_conditional(
        "input.analysis_type === 'Advanced'",
        ui.input_slider("buffer", "Buffer Distance:", 0, 2000, 500),
        ui.input_checkbox("include_transit", "Include Transit", True)
    ),
    
    ui.output_plot("result_map")
)
```
:::

## Dynamic UI

**<span style="color:blue">Generate UI elements based on data</span>**

::: {style="font-size: 85%;"}
```python
from shiny import ui, render

@output
@render.ui
def dynamic_selectors():
    # Get available sensors
    sensors = get_sensors_for_date(input.date())
    
    return ui.input_select(
        "sensor",
        "Available Sensors:",
        choices=sensors
    )
```
:::

## Progress Indicators

::: {style="font-size: 85%;"}
```python
from shiny import ui, render, reactive

@reactive.calc
def long_calculation():
    with ui.Progress(min=0, max=100) as p:
        p.set(message="Loading data...")
        data = load_data()
        
        p.set(25, message="Processing...")
        processed = process_data(data)
        
        p.set(50, message="Analyzing...")
        results = analyze(processed)
        
        p.set(75, message="Creating visualizations...")
        viz = create_viz(results)
        
        p.set(100, message="Complete!")
        return viz
```
:::

# Part 3: Shinylive Deployment

## What is Shinylive?

<div id="contentframe" style="position:absolute; top: 250px; left: 50px;">
<iframe src="https://giphy.com/embed/3o7qE1YN7aBOFPRw8E" width="300" height="200" frameBorder="0" class="giphy-embed" allowFullScreen></iframe>
</div>

::: {style="position:absolute; top: 250px; left: 400px;"}
**<span style="color:blue">Shinylive = Shiny in WebAssembly</span>**

::: {.incremental}
* Runs Python **in your browser**!
* No server needed
* **Free hosting** (GitHub Pages)
* Works offline
* Fast (no server round-trips)
:::
:::

## Traditional vs Shinylive

::: {style="font-size: 85%;"}
**Traditional Shiny:**
```
User Browser → Network → Python Server → Network → Browser
               (slow)        (costs $)      (slow)
```

**Shinylive:**
```
User Browser → WebAssembly Python → Browser
               (all local, fast, free!)
```
:::

## Requirements for Shinylive

::: {.incremental}
1. **Simple file structure** - No complex imports
2. **Single file app** - Or well-organized multi-file
3. **Supported packages** - Most work, some don't
4. **Small data files** - < 5MB recommended
5. **Browser compatible** - Modern browsers only
:::

## What Works in Shinylive? {.smaller}

::: {style="font-size: 85%;"}
**✅ Works great:**
- pandas, numpy, matplotlib
- geopandas, shapely
- ipyleaflet (for maps!)
- seaborn, plotly

**⚠️ Limited or slow:**
- Large file operations
- Computationally intensive tasks
- Database connections

**❌ Doesn't work:**
- osmnx (requires network access)
- r5py (requires Java)
- Some external APIs
:::

## Step 1: Prepare Your App

::: {style="font-size: 90%;"}
```python
# app.py - Your complete app in one file

from shiny import App, ui, render
import pandas as pd
import geopandas as gpd
from ipyleaflet import Map, Marker

# Load data (small files only!)
data = pd.read_csv('pedestrian_data.csv')

app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.h2("My Dashboard"),
    # ... your UI here
)

def server(input, output, session):
    # ... your server logic here
    pass

app = App(app_ui, server)
```
:::

## Step 2: Install Shinylive

::: {style="font-size: 110%;"}
```bash
# In terminal/Colab
pip install shinylive
```

**<span style="color:blue">That's it!</span>** 🎉
:::

## Step 3: Export Your App

::: {style="font-size: 95%;"}
```bash
# Create shinylive export
shinylive export myapp site

# This creates:
site/
├── index.html
├── edit/
└── app files...
```

**<span style="color:blue">The `site` folder can be deployed anywhere!</span>**
:::

## Step 4: Test Locally

::: {style="font-size: 100%;"}
```bash
# Serve locally to test
python -m http.server --directory site 8000

# Open browser to:
# http://localhost:8000
```

**<span style="color:blue">Test everything works before deploying!</span>**
:::

## Step 5: Deploy to GitHub Pages

::: {style="font-size: 85%;"}
**Option 1: Manual deployment**
1. Create GitHub repository
2. Upload `site` folder contents
3. Enable GitHub Pages in Settings
4. URL: `https://yourusername.github.io/reponame`

**Option 2: GitHub Actions (automated)**
```yaml
# .github/workflows/deploy.yml
name: Deploy Shinylive
on: [push]
jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      - run: pip install shinylive
      - run: shinylive export app site
      - uses: peaceiris/actions-gh-pages@v3
        with:
          github_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          publish_dir: ./site
```
:::

## Your Turn: Deploy Simple App

::: {style="font-size: 110%;"}
**<span style="color:blue">Task</span>**: Export and test your dashboard

1. Save app as single `app.py` file
2. Run `shinylive export app site`
3. Test locally with `python -m http.server`
4. If works, deploy to GitHub Pages

**<span style="color:red">Due Thursday!</span>**
:::

## Common Deployment Issues

::: {style="font-size: 85%;"}
**Issue 1: File paths**
```python
# ❌ Wrong - absolute paths don't work
pd.read_csv('/Users/me/data.csv')

# ✅ Correct - relative paths
pd.read_csv('data.csv')
```

**Issue 2: Large files**
```python
# ❌ Wrong - 50MB CSV in repo
pd.read_csv('huge_file.csv')

# ✅ Correct - Load from URL or pre-process
pd.read_csv('https://example.com/data.csv')
```
:::

---

### Common Issues (continued)

::: {style="font-size: 85%;"}
**Issue 3: Package imports**
```python
# ❌ Wrong - packages not in shinylive
import osmnx as ox

# ✅ Correct - use supported packages
import geopandas as gpd
from ipyleaflet import Map
```

**Issue 4: External dependencies**
```python
# ❌ Wrong - requires Java
import r5py

# ✅ Correct - pre-calculate and save results
results = pd.read_csv('precalculated_routes.csv')
```
:::

# Part 4: Assignment 2 Final Requirements

## Technical Requirements Checklist {.smaller}

::: {style="font-size: 85%;"}
**Must have:**
- [ ] Multi-page structure (3+ pages)
- [ ] Interactive map (ipyleaflet or folium)
- [ ] At least 5 different input widgets
- [ ] At least 5 different outputs (plots, tables, maps)
- [ ] Reactive calculations (efficient code)
- [ ] Temporal or comparative analysis
- [ ] Professional styling
- [ ] **Deployed with shinylive**

**Bonus points:**
- [ ] Network analysis integration
- [ ] Advanced reactive patterns
- [ ] Creative visualization
- [ ] User-friendly interface
:::

## Design Requirements

::: {.incremental}
* **<span style="color:blue">Clear navigation</span>** - Easy to find features
* **<span style="color:blue">Consistent layout</span>** - Similar pages look similar
* **<span style="color:blue">Readable text</span>** - Good font sizes, contrast
* **<span style="color:blue">Responsive feedback</span>** - Loading indicators
* **<span style="color:blue">Error handling</span>** - Helpful messages
:::

## Evaluation Criteria

::: {style="font-size: 85%;"}
| Criterion | Weight | What we're looking for |
|-----------|--------|------------------------|
| **Functionality** | 40% | All features work, no crashes |
| **Design** | 20% | Professional, intuitive, clear |
| **Analysis** | 20% | Meaningful insights, good visualizations |
| **Technical** | 15% | Clean code, efficient, well-structured |
| **Deployment** | 5% | Works in shinylive, accessible |

**Total: 35% of final grade**
:::

## Example: Excellent Dashboard

![](https://shiny.posit.co/r/gallery/interactive-visualizations/crime-explorer/thumbnail.png){fig-align=center width=60%}

::: {style="font-size: 85%;"}
**Why it's good:**
* Clear purpose and title
* Intuitive controls on left
* Large, clear visualization
* Multiple linked views
* Professional appearance
:::

## Testing Your Dashboard

::: {style="font-size: 90%;"}
**Before submission, test:**

1. **All inputs work** - Try every slider, selector, button
2. **All outputs update** - Check reactivity
3. **Edge cases** - What if no data selected?
4. **Different browsers** - Chrome, Firefox, Safari
5. **Mobile view** - Does it work on phone?
6. **Fresh eyes** - Ask a friend to try it
:::

## Debugging Tips {.smaller}

::: {style="font-size: 85%;"}
**<span style="color:blue">Use print statements</span>**
```python
@reactive.calc
def filtered_data():
    print(f"Filter called with: {input.sensor()}")  # Debug
    data = filter_function()
    print(f"Returned {len(data)} rows")  # Debug
    return data
```

**<span style="color:blue">Check browser console</span>**
* Right-click → Inspect → Console
* Look for JavaScript errors

**<span style="color:blue">Test components separately</span>**
```python
# Test your functions outside Shiny first
data = pd.read_csv('test.csv')
result = your_analysis_function(data)
print(result)  # Does it work?
```
:::

## Time Management

::: {style="font-size: 90%;"}
**<span style="color:red">Due in 2 days!</span>**

**Today (Tuesday):**
- Finalize all features
- Test everything
- Fix bugs

**Wednesday:**
- Polish design
- Export with shinylive
- Deploy to GitHub Pages
- Test deployed version

**Thursday morning:**
- Final checks
- Submit to Canvas
:::

## Getting Help

::: {.incremental}
* **<span style="color:blue">Office hours</span>**: Today and tomorrow
* **<span style="color:blue">Ed discussion</span>**: Post questions
* **<span style="color:blue">Lab sessions</span>**: TAs available
* **<span style="color:blue">Classmates</span>**: Help each other (but don't copy!)
:::

## Summary

::: {.incremental}
* **Temporal analysis** - Time sliders, date ranges
* **Reactive patterns** - `@reactive.calc`, `@reactive.event`
* **Shinylive deployment** - Export, test, deploy
* **Assignment 2** - Due Thursday, 35% of grade
* **You can do this!** 🚀
:::

## Next Week

**<span style="color:blue">Week 9: Python Package Development</span>**

::: {.incremental}
* Micromobility and urban analytics
* Why package your code?
* Building your first package
* Assignment 3 begins (30% + 10% poster = 40%)
:::

## Final Tips for Assignment 2

::: {.incremental}
* **<span style="color:blue">Keep it simple</span>** - Working is better than fancy but broken
* **<span style="color:blue">Test early, test often</span>** - Don't wait until Wednesday night!
* **<span style="color:blue">Document your choices</span>** - Comment your code
* **<span style="color:blue">Make it yours</span>** - Show creativity and insight
* **<span style="color:blue">Have fun!</span>** - This is your chance to build something real
:::

## Resources

* Shiny for Python: shiny.posit.co/py
* Shinylive docs: shiny.posit.co/py/docs/shinylive.html
* ipyleaflet: ipyleaflet.readthedocs.io
* GitHub Pages: pages.github.com

## Thanks! <br> Q & A {style="text-align: center;"}

**Good luck with Assignment 2!**

You've got this! 💪

<div style="width:840px"><iframe allow="fullscreen" frameBorder="0" height="480" src="https://giphy.com/embed/3oz8xAFtqoOUUrsh7W/video" width="840"></iframe></div>
